Indicates whether this profile record is the surviving (golden) record following a guest profile merge operation. True = this is the authoritative survivor record; False = this is a source or non-survivor record. Critical for identity resolution and deduplication governance.

The professional job title of the guest as provided during profile creation or CRM synchronization. Supports corporate account management, MICE event coordination, and personalized service delivery for business travelers.
The timestamp of the most recent modification to the guest profile record in the source system. Used for change data capture (CDC), ETL incremental loads, and audit trail compliance.
The date on which the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Used to calculate member tenure, tier qualification windows, and anniversary recognition benefits.

The guest's loyalty program membership number assigned by the central loyalty platform (Salesforce CRM / loyalty engine). The primary identifier linking the guest profile to loyalty tier, points balance, and rewards history.

The guest's current loyalty program tier level, determining benefit eligibility, upgrade priority, and service recognition protocols. Tiers progress from NONE (non-member) through SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM, to DIAMOND (highest).

Indicates whether the guest has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications (email, SMS, direct mail). True = opted in; False = opted out or no consent given. Mandatory for GDPR and CCPA compliance in marketing campaign execution.
The guest's middle name or initial as provided. Supports full legal name matching for identity verification, passport validation, and regulatory compliance.
The guest's mobile/cell phone number in E.164 format. Used for SMS pre-arrival messaging, mobile check-in notifications, and digital key delivery.

Honorific or salutation prefix for the guest (e.g., Mr, Mrs, Dr). Used in personalized correspondence, folio headers, and VIP recognition communications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Mr|Mrs|Ms|Miss|Dr|Prof|Sir|Rev — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Name suffix for the guest (e.g., Jr., Sr., III, PhD). Supports full legal name representation for identity documents and formal correspondence.
The guest's nationality expressed as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Required for regulatory reporting, visa compliance, and international guest analytics.

The native guest profile identifier assigned by Oracle OPERA Property Management System (PMS). Used for cross-system reconciliation and source-system traceability back to the PMS guest profile record.
The expiration date of the guest's passport document. Used to validate document currency at check-in and for regulatory compliance in jurisdictions requiring valid travel document verification.

business_glossary_term: Guest Passport Issuing Country Code (ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3)
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the authority that issued the guest's passport. Required for immigration compliance and international guest regulatory reporting.

The guest's passport document number as presented at check-in. Required for international guest identity verification, regulatory reporting to local authorities, and compliance with immigration laws in applicable jurisdictions.
The primary contact phone number for the guest in E.164 international format. Used for reservation confirmations, pre-arrival contact, and service recovery outreach.
The guest's preferred bed configuration for room assignment: KING, QUEEN, DOUBLE, TWIN, or SINGLE. Used during pre-arrival room blocking and at check-in to maximize guest satisfaction.

The guest's preferred floor level or floor range for room assignment (e.g., high floor, low floor, specific floor number). Captured from guest preferences in OPERA PMS and used during pre-arrival room blocking.

The guest's preferred communication language expressed as an ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., en, fr, de, zh). Drives language selection for folios, correspondence, digital communications, and on-property service delivery.

Current lifecycle state of the guest master profile: active (in use), inactive (dormant, no recent stays), merged (absorbed into a survivor record), suspended (temporarily blocked), deceased (confirmed deceased, retained for audit). Governs record visibility and processing eligibility.

The guest's smoking preference for room assignment: NON_SMOKING, SMOKING (where available), or NO_PREFERENCE. Used during pre-arrival room blocking and check-in assignment to comply with guest preferences and property smoking policies.

Granular consent flag specifically for SMS/text message marketing communications. Supports channel-specific consent management as required by GDPR, CCPA, and TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act) regulations.

The VIP designation level assigned to the guest in Oracle OPERA PMS, indicating the tier of elevated service protocols to apply at check-in and throughout the stay. NONE=standard guest, VIP1-VIP3=escalating VIP tiers, VVIP=highest recognition, BLACKLIST=do-not-accommodate flag.
Unique surrogate identifier for a guest preference record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the preference data product within the guest domain.
Reference to the guest profile to whom this preference record belongs. Links the preference to the unified guest identity in the guest domain.
Reference to the property at which this preference was captured or is applicable. A null value indicates the preference applies globally across all properties for the guest.

Foreign key linking to inventory.room. Business justification: VIP and repeat-guest specific-room preference tracking: high-value guests request the same physical room on every stay (e.g., suite 1201). The pre-arrival assignment process and VIP checklist depend o

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Pre-arrival room assignment process: the system matches guest room-type preferences to available inventory. A hospitality operations expert expects preference records to reference the specific room_ty

Reference to the specific stay or reservation during which this preference was captured or last confirmed. Enables preference-to-stay linkage for analytics on preference fulfillment rates and guest satisfaction correlation.

Specific description of the guest's food or environmental allergy (e.g., 'severe peanut allergy - anaphylactic', 'shellfish allergy', 'latex allergy'). Classified as restricted health information under GDPR and CCPA. Triggers mandatory F&B and housekeeping alerts. Only populated when is_allergy = True.

Guest's stated preferences for in-room amenities and welcome items (e.g., 'extra towels', 'foam bath products', 'specific brand toiletries', 'fruit basket', 'champagne on arrival'). Free-form text to accommodate the wide variety of amenity requests. Communicated to housekeeping and concierge for pre-arrival setup.

Guest's stated preference for bed configuration in their room. Sourced from OPERA PMS guest profile and used during room assignment to match available inventory. Supports pre-arrival preparation and upsell opportunity identification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: king|queen|double|twin|single|rollaway|no_preference — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

High-level category classifying the type of guest preference. Drives routing to the appropriate operational team (e.g., housekeeping, F&B, front desk). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: room|bed|pillow|amenity|dietary|housekeeping|temperature|newspaper|accessibility|fnb|transportation|communication|loyalty|spa — promote to reference product]

Standardized alphanumeric code representing the preference value as defined in the Property Management System (OPERA PMS) preference code table. Used for system-to-system integration and operational workflow routing (e.g., 'KGBED', 'NONSM', 'OCNVW', 'VEGAN').

Guest's preferred channel for receiving hotel communications including pre-arrival messages, in-stay notifications, and post-stay follow-up. Drives marketing and operational communication routing in Salesforce CRM. Supports GDPR and CCPA consent management.

Date on which the guest provided consent for collection and use of this preference data. Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trails. Null if consent_given is False or consent was not explicitly recorded.

Indicates whether the guest has provided explicit consent for the collection and use of this preference data (True). Required for GDPR Article 7 and CCPA compliance. Preferences captured without consent must not be used for marketing personalization.
Timestamp when this preference record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR Article 30 records of processing activities compliance.

Data governance classification level for this preference record, aligned with the enterprise data classification policy. 'restricted' for health/allergy data, 'confidential' for personal preferences, 'internal' for operational preferences. Drives access control and data handling policies.

Guest's stated dietary restriction or special dietary requirement (e.g., 'vegetarian', 'vegan', 'halal', 'kosher', 'gluten-free', 'lactose-free', 'diabetic'). Communicated to F&B operations via MICROS POS and Delphi for event catering. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vegetarian|vegan|halal|kosher|gluten_free|lactose_free|diabetic|low_sodium|nut_free|shellfish_free — promote to reference product]

Free-text notes from operational staff regarding the fulfillment of this preference, including reasons for non-fulfillment, substitutions made, or special circumstances. Supports service recovery workflows and guest history documentation.

Operational status indicating whether this preference was fulfilled during the most recent stay. 'pending' = not yet actioned; 'fulfilled' = successfully delivered; 'partially_fulfilled' = partially met; 'not_available' = could not be accommodated (e.g., room type unavailable); 'waived' = guest waived the preference on arrival.

Guest's preferred timing and frequency for housekeeping service. Communicated to the housekeeping management system to schedule room servicing. 'do_not_disturb' and 'checkout_only' options support sustainability programs and guest privacy preferences.

business_glossary_term: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Requirement Flag
description: Indicates whether this preference represents an ADA-mandated accessibility accommodation (True), such as roll-in shower, hearing-accessible room, visual alert devices, or accessible room location. ADA requirements carry legal compliance obligations and must be fulfilled without substitution.

Indicates whether this preference record represents a food or environmental allergy (True). Allergy records trigger mandatory F&B and housekeeping alerts and are subject to heightened fulfillment controls per ISO 22000 food safety standards.

Indicates whether this preference is a hard requirement that must be fulfilled (True) or a soft preference that should be accommodated when possible (False). Mandatory preferences include ADA accessibility requirements, severe food allergies, and medically necessary accommodations.

Guest's preferred language for communications, in-room materials, and staff interactions. Stored as BCP 47 language tag (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'es', 'zh-CN', 'de'). Used to personalize pre-arrival communications, in-room collateral, and staff greeting protocols.

Date on which the guest most recently confirmed this preference remains accurate and current. Used to identify stale preferences that may need re-validation during pre-arrival contact or check-in. Supports GDPR data accuracy principle.

The guest's loyalty program tier at the time this preference was captured (e.g., 'Silver', 'Gold', 'Platinum', 'Diamond'). Provides context for preference prioritization and fulfillment SLA — higher-tier guests may receive elevated preference fulfillment commitments.

Guest's preferred newspaper or publication for complimentary delivery (e.g., 'The Wall Street Journal', 'The New York Times', 'Financial Times', 'USA Today', 'local paper'). Used by front desk and concierge for pre-arrival preparation and morning delivery scheduling.

Guest's stated preference for pillow type and firmness. Sourced from OPERA PMS guest profile and communicated to housekeeping for pre-arrival room preparation. Hypoallergenic pillow requests may overlap with allergy flags. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: firm|soft|medium|feather|hypoallergenic|foam|no_preference — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Current lifecycle status of the preference record. 'active' indicates the preference is valid and should be honored; 'inactive' indicates it has been withdrawn or expired; 'pending_verification' indicates it requires staff confirmation; 'superseded' indicates it has been replaced by a newer preference record.

Specific type of preference within the category (e.g., 'floor_level', 'bed_type', 'smoking_status', 'view_type', 'pillow_firmness', 'dietary_restriction', 'allergy', 'newspaper_title', 'housekeeping_time', 'room_temperature'). Provides granular classification for operational fulfillment and personalization analytics.

Numeric rank indicating the relative importance of this preference when multiple preferences of the same type exist for a guest. Lower values indicate higher priority (1 = highest). Used by pre-arrival preparation workflows to resolve conflicts.

Guest's stated preference for the floor level of their room assignment. Used by front desk and room assignment workflows in OPERA PMS to optimize room allocation during check-in and pre-arrival room blocking.

Guest's preferred room temperature setting in degrees Celsius. Used for pre-arrival room preparation and smart room automation where available. Stored in Celsius for standardization; display conversion to Fahrenheit handled at the presentation layer.

Guest's preference for smoking or non-smoking room designation. Critical for room assignment compliance and property policy adherence. Most properties are entirely non-smoking; this field captures exceptions and guest-stated preferences for reporting.

Indicates how the preference was captured: 'stated' (guest explicitly requested), 'inferred' (derived from stay history patterns), 'observed' (noted by staff during stay), 'crm_imported' (sourced from Salesforce CRM), 'loyalty_profile' (from loyalty program record), 'survey' (from Medallia feedback), 'service_recovery' (captured during a service recovery interaction). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stated|inferred|observed|crm_imported|loyalty_profile|survey|service_recovery — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The native identifier of this preference record in the originating source system (e.g., OPERA PMS preference record ID, Salesforce CRM preference object ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit.
Timestamp when this preference record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.

Date after which this preference record expires and should no longer be honored. Null indicates the preference is open-ended with no expiry. Supports GDPR data minimisation requirements by enabling automatic expiry of time-limited preferences.

The stated or inferred value of the preference (e.g., 'high floor', 'king bed', 'non-smoking', 'ocean view', 'firm pillow', 'vegetarian', 'gluten-free', 'The Wall Street Journal', 'do not disturb before 10am', '72°F'). Free-form string to accommodate the wide variety of preference types.

Guest's stated preference for the type of view from their room. Used during room assignment and pre-arrival blocking to enhance guest satisfaction. Supports upsell workflows when preferred view type is available at a premium. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocean|pool|garden|city|mountain|courtyard|no_preference — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Date from which this preference record is effective and should be honored during guest stays. Supports time-bound preferences (e.g., a temporary dietary restriction during a medical recovery period) and preference versioning.
Unique identifier for the guest market segment record. Primary key for both segment master definitions and individual guest-to-segment assignment records.

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Guest segmentation must map to revenue market segmentation for rate strategy alignment, RMS rule targeting, and segment-level budget planning. Revenue managers reconcile guest segment assignments agai
Reference to the parent segment in the hierarchy for roll-up aggregation. Null for top-level segments.
Reference to the segment assignment record that supersedes this one. Used for tracking segment migration history and maintaining assignment lineage.
Reference to the individual guest profile for guest-to-segment assignment records. Null for segment master definition records.

Foreign key linking to property.property. Business justification: Revenue managers configure market segments at the property level — segment ADR index, RevPAR contribution, and yield management flags are property-specific. Property-level segment reporting (e.g., seg

business_glossary_term: ADR (Average Daily Rate) Index vs Property
description: Average Daily Rate index for this segment relative to property-wide ADR. 1.0 = at property average, >1.0 = premium segment, <1.0 = discount segment. Used for segment mix optimization.
Typical advance booking window in days for this segment. Used for demand forecasting and pickup analysis. Transient segments typically 0-30 days, Group segments 90-365 days.
Average ancillary revenue (F&B, spa, parking, resort fees, etc.) per stay for this segment. Used for total revenue forecasting and TRevPAR optimization.
Confidence score (0.000 to 1.000) for ML-inferred segment assignments. Higher scores indicate greater model certainty. Null for rule-based and manual assignments.
Date when the guest was assigned to this segment. Used for tracking segment migration and guest lifecycle analysis.
Date from which this segment assignment is effective for the guest. Used for time-bound segment membership (e.g., corporate contract validity period).
Date when this segment assignment expires for the guest. Null for indefinite assignments. Used for contract-based and time-limited segment memberships.

Method used to assign the guest to this segment. Rule-based uses business rules (e.g., booking channel, rate code), ML-inferred uses machine learning models for predictive segmentation, Manual-override represents analyst or sales manager assignment, Reservation-based inherits from reservation market segment code.
Free-text notes explaining the rationale for manual segment assignments or overrides. Used for audit trail and knowledge transfer.
Code indicating the business reason for this segment assignment (e.g., CORP_CONTRACT, BOOKING_CHANNEL, STAY_PATTERN, SALES_OVERRIDE, LTV_TIER). Used for assignment audit and quality control.
Historical average Length of Stay in days for guests in this segment. Used for demand forecasting and inventory optimization.
Code identifying the cancellation and modification policy applicable to this segment (e.g., 24HR, 72HR, NRR for Non-Refundable Rate, FLEX).
Broader business category for revenue and demand analysis. Used for RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) decomposition and TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) attribution.
Short alphanumeric code identifying the market segment (e.g., TRANS, GRP, CORP, WHSL, COMP, CREW). Sourced from OPERA PMS Market Segment codes and IDeaS G3 RMS segment taxonomy.
Indicates whether bookings in this segment are eligible for travel agent or OTA commission payments.
Standard commission percentage paid to intermediaries for bookings in this segment. Null if commission_eligible is False.
Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the lakehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
Indicates whether group bookings in this segment should undergo displacement analysis to compare group revenue against potential transient revenue. Used for group evaluation and acceptance decisions.
Date when this segment definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active segments.
Date when this segment definition became effective. Used for historical segment analysis and time-travel queries.

business_glossary_term: F&B (Food and Beverage) Attachment Rate Percentage
description: Percentage of guests in this segment who purchase F&B services during their stay. Used for TRevPAR analysis and ancillary revenue forecasting.

Numeric level in the segment hierarchy for roll-up reporting. Level 1 is top-level (e.g., Transient), Level 2 is sub-segment (e.g., Qualified Corporate), Level 3 is micro-segment (e.g., Tech Industry Corporate).
Timestamp when this segment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.
Indicates whether stays booked under this segment earn loyalty program points and elite night credits.
Multiplier applied to base points earning for this segment. Standard is 1.0, promotional segments may have higher multipliers (e.g., 1.5x, 2.0x).

Indicates whether this segment has Last Room Availability commitment, meaning inventory is available at the contracted rate even when the property is sold out. Typically True for top-tier corporate contracts and loyalty elite members.
Username or system identifier of the user or process that last modified this record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Full descriptive name of the market segment (e.g., Transient, Group, Corporate Negotiated, Wholesale, Complimentary, Airline Crew).

Pricing strategy applicable to this segment. Dynamic uses RMS (Revenue Management System) optimization, Fixed uses BAR (Best Available Rate) or published rates, Negotiated uses contracted rates, Promotional uses discount codes, Opaque uses non-branded OTA channels.

business_glossary_term: RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) Contribution Percentage
description: Historical percentage contribution of this segment to total property RevPAR. Sum across all segments equals 100%. Used for strategic segment mix planning.

Current lifecycle status of the segment definition. Active segments are available for assignment and reporting, Inactive segments are hidden from selection but retained for historical analysis, Deprecated segments are being phased out, Pending segments are awaiting approval.

High-level classification of the segment type. Transient represents FIT (Free Independent Traveler) guests, Group represents MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions) and leisure groups, Contract represents negotiated corporate accounts, Wholesale represents OTA (Online Travel Agency) and tour operator bookings, Complimentary represents comp stays and employee rates, Crew represents airline and travel industry staff.
Unique identifier of this segment in the source system. Used for data reconciliation and lineage tracking.

business_glossary_term: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) Mapping Code
description: Standard USALI account code for financial reporting and GOP (Gross Operating Profit) attribution. Ensures consistent revenue classification across properties.
Indicates whether this segment is subject to dynamic yield optimization and inventory controls by the RMS. True for transient and qualified segments, False for contracted and complimentary segments.
Unique surrogate identifier for each completed guest stay record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the stay_history data product. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS post-checkout records.

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Channel production reporting, loyalty points attribution by channel, and OTA cost-of-acquisition analysis all require stay_history to reference the exact booking_source. booking_channel_code is a deno

Reference to the corporate account associated with this stay, applicable when guest_type is 'corporate'. Links to negotiated rate agreements and corporate travel program tracking. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS accounts receivable module.

Reference to the group block associated with this stay, applicable when guest_type is 'group'. Links to group contract, pickup tracking, and group revenue attribution. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS group module.

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Each stay must be attributed to a revenue market segment for USALI segment-level P&L reporting, RevPAR contribution analysis, and performance_actuals reconciliation. Revenue managers depend on this li

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Points posting and member stay history reporting require linking each stay to the loyalty member record. Loyalty operations analysts reconcile points earned per stay against the member account. loyalt
Reference to the guest profile record representing the primary registered guest for this stay. Supports Lifetime Value (LTV) calculation, segmentation, and personalization across all properties.
Reference to the property where the stay occurred. Enables property-level performance analytics including ADR, RevPAR, and OCC reporting per USALI standards.
Reference to the originating reservation record from which this stay history was created upon checkout. Links stay history back to the reservation domain for full booking-to-stay traceability.

Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Linking stay history to the rate plan applied enables rate plan performance analysis (ADR by rate plan, LRA compliance tracking, rate plan pickup reporting). Revenue managers need this to evaluate whi

Foreign key linking to inventory.room. Business justification: Physical room occupancy history supports maintenance correlation with guest complaints, VIP room preference tracking at the physical room level, and housekeeping performance reporting. room_number is

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Historical stay reporting by room category, loyalty tier qualification based on room type upgrades, and RevPAR contribution analysis all require linking stay history to the room_type entity. room_type

Average Daily Rate (ADR) for the stay, calculated as total room revenue divided by the number of occupied room nights. A primary KPI per USALI and STR STAR Report standards. Expressed in the property's local currency. Used for RevPAR computation and competitive benchmarking via STR.

Total ancillary charges posted to the guest folio during the stay, including spa, parking, laundry, telephone, and other non-room, non-F&B charges. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS. Contributes to TRevPAR and GOPPAR calculations per USALI.

Actual date the guest checked in to the property (yyyy-MM-dd). May differ from the originally reserved arrival date in cases of early arrival or late arrival. Used as the principal business event date for stay analytics and STR benchmarking.

Date on which the reservation was originally created (yyyy-MM-dd). Used to calculate booking lead time (days between booking_date and arrival_date), a key input for demand forecasting and pickup report analysis.

Exact date and time the guest completed the check-in process at the front desk or via mobile/digital check-in (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS front desk module. Used for operational SLA tracking and guest experience analytics.

Exact date and time the guest completed the checkout process (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Marks the point at which the stay record is finalized and posted to the stay history.

Indicates whether the stay was provided on a complimentary (no-charge) basis (True = complimentary; False = revenue-generating stay). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for USALI complimentary room reporting and GOP calculation.

Externally-known alphanumeric confirmation number assigned by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS) at time of booking. Used by guests and agents to reference the stay. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS or Oracle OPERA PMS.

Timestamp when this stay history record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse, typically upon post-checkout processing from Oracle OPERA PMS (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and ETL pipeline monitoring.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this stay record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency properties and international financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.

Actual date the guest checked out of the property (yyyy-MM-dd). May differ from the originally reserved departure date in cases of early departure or extended stay. Used in conjunction with arrival_date to compute Length of Stay (LOS).

Indicates whether the guest activated Do Not Disturb (DND) status during the stay (True = DND activated at any point; False = DND not activated). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS housekeeping module. Used for housekeeping scheduling and guest preference analytics.

Total Food and Beverage (F&B) charges posted to the guest folio during the stay, including restaurant, bar, room service, and minibar charges. Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS. Contributes to TRevPAR calculation.
Topics extracted from review/feedback for the stay

Overall Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) for the stay as captured by Medallia post-stay survey, typically on a scale of 1-10 or 1-100 depending on survey instrument. Distinct from NPS; measures overall satisfaction rather than likelihood to recommend. Used for Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking (SALT) reporting.

Classification of the guest stay by traveler category. 'fit' (Free Independent Traveler) indicates leisure individual; 'corporate' indicates business traveler under a negotiated account; 'group' indicates part of a group block; 'crew' indicates airline or transport crew; 'complimentary' indicates no-charge stay. Drives segmentation and LTV modeling.

Actual number of nights the guest stayed at the property, computed as departure_date minus arrival_date. A core metric for Average Length of Stay (ALOS) analysis, revenue management, and demand forecasting in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Number of loyalty program points earned by the guest for this stay, based on the applicable earning rate for the rate plan, room type, and member tier. Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management. Used for loyalty program liability accrual and member engagement analytics.

The guest's loyalty program tier level at the time of the stay (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond). Captured as a snapshot to preserve historical tier context for LTV analysis and personalization, independent of the guest's current tier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product as tiers vary by brand]

Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the guest for this stay, on a scale of 0-10. Sourced from Medallia post-stay survey. Used for Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) tracking, service recovery identification, and experience analytics. Null if guest did not respond to survey.
Count of adult guests (18 years and older) registered for the stay as recorded in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for occupancy reporting, F&B revenue forecasting, and capacity planning.
Count of child guests (under 18 years) registered for the stay as recorded in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for family segment analytics, amenity planning, and F&B revenue forecasting.
The arrival date as originally reserved at time of booking, before any modifications. Used to measure early arrival or late arrival deviations and to support wash factor analysis in IDeaS G3 RMS.

The departure date as originally reserved at time of booking, before any modifications. Used to identify early departure and extended stay patterns for demand forecasting and wash factor analysis in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Primary payment instrument used to settle the guest folio at checkout. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Used for payment mix analysis and PCI DSS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|direct_bill|loyalty_points|bank_transfer|voucher|cryptocurrency — promote to reference product]

Number of nights from this stay that count toward the guest's loyalty program tier qualification. May differ from los_nights if the rate plan is non-qualifying (e.g., wholesale, complimentary). Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management.

Code identifying the rate plan under which the stay was booked (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate negotiated, package). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. Critical for revenue management analysis and channel contribution reporting.

Total room charges posted to the guest folio for the entire stay, excluding taxes, fees, and ancillary charges. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Core component of RevPAR and TRevPAR calculations per USALI.

Indicates whether a service recovery action was initiated during or after this stay (True = service recovery case opened; False = no service recovery). Sourced from Medallia or Salesforce CRM case management. Used for Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) calculation and experience analytics.

Outcome status of the guest stay upon checkout processing. 'completed' indicates normal checkout on scheduled departure date; 'early_departure' indicates guest checked out before scheduled departure; 'extended' indicates stay was prolonged beyond original departure date; 'no_show' indicates reservation was not cancelled but guest never arrived; 'cancelled' indicates reservation was cancelled post-check-in processing.

Total tax charges applied to the guest folio for the stay, including occupancy tax, VAT, city tax, and other applicable levies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering. Required for IFRS/GAAP financial reporting and SOX compliance.

Total amount charged to the guest folio for the entire stay, inclusive of room revenue, F&B revenue, ancillary revenue, and taxes. Represents the gross billing amount settled at checkout. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module.

Timestamp when this stay history record was last modified in the silver layer lakehouse (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Captures post-checkout adjustments such as folio corrections, survey score updates, or loyalty point recalculations. Used for data lineage and audit compliance.

VIP designation code assigned to the guest for this stay in Oracle OPERA PMS (e.g., VIP1, VIP2, VVIP, CELEB). Drives special handling, amenity delivery, and management attention protocols. Null if guest has no VIP designation.

Unique surrogate identifier for each VIP designation record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the vip_designation data product. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS VIP code assignments and Salesforce CRM VIP profile records.

Reference to the corporate account record when the VIP designation is granted based on a corporate relationship (e.g., key account executive, contracted corporate partner). Null for individual leisure VIP guests. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account object.

Reference to the loyalty program membership record associated with this VIP guest. Links VIP designation entitlements with loyalty tier benefits to ensure non-duplication and correct benefit stacking. Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management module.

Foreign key linking to inventory.room. Business justification: VIP pre-arrival room pre-blocking: the VIP designation record drives automatic reservation of a specific physical room. room_assignment_priority and pre_arrival_checklist_template on vip_designation r

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: VIP pre-arrival room blocking process: the system must know which room type a VIP guest is guaranteed or preferred for. upgrade_eligible and room_assignment_priority on vip_designation require a targe

Reference to the guest profile record for whom this VIP designation is assigned. Links to the master guest profile in the guest domain. A single guest may hold multiple concurrent VIP designations across brand and property levels.

Reference to the property at which this VIP designation applies. A designation may be property-specific (e.g., Celebrity at a single resort) or brand-wide (null indicates brand-level designation). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: VIP designation assignment workflows are directly driven by loyalty tier — Platinum members automatically receive VIP-1 status. Guest services and front-office systems use this link to apply tier-appr

Indicates whether a complimentary or arranged airport transfer service is required for this VIP guest as part of their designation entitlements. Triggers coordination with the concierge and transportation team during pre-arrival preparation.

Pseudonym or alias name used for incognito check-in and reservation bookings to protect the identity of high-profile guests. Used in place of the guest's legal name on folios, room assignments, and staff communications when incognito_checkin is true.

Code referencing the amenity entitlement package associated with this VIP level. Determines the standard amenity setup (e.g., welcome fruit basket, champagne, floral arrangement, personalized stationery) to be prepared prior to guest arrival. Sourced from OPERA PMS amenity configuration.

Number of active VIP designations currently held by this guest across all scopes (brand and property level). Supports operational awareness that a guest may hold multiple concurrent designations (e.g., VIP3 at brand level and Celebrity at property level) and ensures all applicable protocols are honored.

Timestamp when this VIP designation record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse. Represents the audit trail creation event. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions.

Externally-known alphanumeric code identifying this VIP designation record, as assigned in Oracle OPERA PMS VIP code table (e.g., VIP1, VIP3, CELEB, ROYAL, OWNER). Used for cross-system reference and pre-arrival checklist triggers.

Internal narrative notes providing additional context about the VIP designation, such as the relationship history, specific events that triggered the designation, or instructions for the guest relations team. Visible only to authorized staff. Sourced from Salesforce CRM case notes.

Business rationale for granting the VIP designation. Revenue threshold indicates the guest meets a minimum annual spend threshold. Corporate relationship indicates a key account or contracted corporate partner. Public figure, diplomatic, and celebrity reasons trigger additional privacy protocols. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: revenue_threshold|corporate_relationship|public_figure|loyalty_elite|owner|board_member|celebrity|diplomatic|media|government — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether the VIP designation applies across the entire brand portfolio, is restricted to a specific property, or applies to a regional cluster of properties. Drives which properties must honor the designation during pre-arrival preparation.

Current lifecycle state of the VIP designation record. Active designations drive pre-arrival checklists and upgrade eligibility. Suspended designations are temporarily paused. Revoked designations have been withdrawn due to policy violation or relationship change.

Indicates that the guest has a standing do-not-disturb preference that must be honored by housekeeping, engineering, and all operational departments unless a safety emergency arises. Overrides standard housekeeping scheduling in Oracle OPERA PMS.

Calendar date on which the VIP designation becomes active and service protocols are triggered. Used to schedule pre-arrival preparation and amenity entitlements. Aligns with OPERA PMS VIP code validity start date.

Calendar date on which the VIP designation expires and service protocols are deactivated. Null indicates an open-ended designation with no planned expiry. Annual review cycles typically reset this date. Aligns with OPERA PMS VIP code validity end date.

Indicates whether the General Manager (GM) or designated senior property leader is required to personally greet this VIP guest upon arrival. Triggers a GM greeting task in the pre-arrival preparation checklist within Oracle OPERA PMS.

Indicates that the guest requires a discreet, incognito check-in process to protect their identity and privacy. When true, the front desk must use a private check-in area, suppress the guest name from public-facing displays, and limit staff awareness of the guest's presence. Critical for celebrity and diplomatic guests.

Date of the guest's most recent completed stay at any property under this VIP designation's scope. Used during annual VIP review cycles to assess continued eligibility based on recency of engagement. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS reservation history.

Indicates that all media inquiries, press requests, and social media references related to this guest's stay must be suppressed and escalated to the Director of Communications. Distinct from no_photo_policy as it governs external communications rather than on-property photography.

Classification of the segment's sensitivity to rate changes. Inelastic segments (e.g., airline crew, government contracted) book regardless of rate fluctuations. Highly elastic segments (e.g., leisure OTA) respond strongly to price changes. Drives DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) configuration in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Classification of the rate plan type typically associated with this segment. BAR (Best Available Rate) for transient leisure, negotiated for corporate accounts, LRA (Last Room Availability) for top-tier corporate, NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) for advance purchase segments. Drives rate loading in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|negotiated|lra|nrr|wholesale|package|complimentary — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Primary revenue category associated with this market segment for TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) and USALI departmental reporting. Rooms-focused segments contribute primarily to rooms revenue; MICE segments contribute across rooms, F&B (Food and Beverage), and events revenue buckets.

The segment code as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Used for demand forecasting, pricing optimization, and inventory control at the segment level. May differ from PMS segment code due to system-specific taxonomy requirements.

High-level classification grouping the segment into a primary demand category. Drives pricing strategy, channel allocation, and revenue reporting roll-ups. Aligns with STR segment category definitions used in STAR Report benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|contract|wholesale|ota|corporate|government|leisure|crew|airline — promote to reference product]

Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the market segment, used as the externally-known business identifier across Oracle OPERA PMS, IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Infor EzRMS. Examples: CORP, TRANS, LEIS, OTA, GRP, GOVT, CREW. Aligns with STR segment code taxonomy for benchmarking and STAR Report mapping.

Full descriptive name of the market segment as displayed in reporting, dashboards, and revenue management systems. Examples: Corporate Negotiated, Leisure Transient, Online Travel Agency, Government/Military, Group Meetings. Used as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this resource entity.

Secondary classification distinguishing whether the segment represents individual (FIT — Free Independent Traveler) bookings, group blocks, negotiated corporate accounts, wholesale/tour operator contracts, or consortia arrangements. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS for demand segmentation and pricing strategy.

Numeric value controlling the display sequence of market segments in reports, dashboards, and system configuration screens. Ensures consistent ordering in Oracle OPERA PMS segment lists, IDeaS G3 RMS segment configuration, and BI reporting tools.

The official STR (Smith Travel Research) segment code used for competitive benchmarking and STAR Report alignment. Enables mapping of internal segment definitions to the industry-standard STR taxonomy for RGI (Revenue Generation Index), MPI (Market Penetration Index), and ARI (Average Rate Index) calculations.

Timestamp when this market segment record was last modified. Tracks configuration changes to segment definitions, status updates, and taxonomy revisions. Essential for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines feeding IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS.

business_glossary_term: Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry (USALI) Segment Code
description: The USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) standardized segment code used for financial reporting and departmental P&L allocation. Ensures consistency in GOP (Gross Operating Profit), NOI (Net Operating Income), and EBITDA reporting across properties and for external financial audits under GAAP/IFRS.
Primary key for performance_actuals

Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_budget. Business justification: Performance actuals carry denormalized budget targets (budget_adr, budget_occupancy_rate, budget_room_revenue, budget_total_revenue) for variance computation. Adding revenue_budget_id normalizes these

Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: All performance_actuals monetary KPIs (ADR, RevPAR, GOP, EBITDA) are currency-denominated. currency_code is denormalized; FK to currency enforces 3NF and is required for STR benchmarking, owner report

Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: Performance actuals are compared against demand forecasts for MAPE (forecast accuracy) and variance analysis — a core function of IDeaS G3 RMS and STR STAR reporting. Adding demand_forecast_id links e

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Daily actuals track which room nights and revenue came from specific event bookings for segment reporting. Required for event contribution analysis, MICE segment performance reporting, and group vs tr

Foreign key linking to property.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Franchise fee calculations (royalty, marketing, loyalty fees) are computed from performance actuals revenue figures. Linking performance_actuals to franchise_agreement enables automated franchise fee

Foreign key linking to property.hierarchy. Business justification: Performance actuals are reported and benchmarked at hierarchy node levels (brand, region, cluster) for executive dashboards and STR market reporting. Linking to hierarchy enables hierarchical KPI roll

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Daily actuals track outlet-level F&B revenue for TRevPAR calculation and USALI reporting. Required for total property revenue reporting, GOP analysis, outlet performance attribution in consolidated fi

Foreign key linking to loyalty.promotion. Business justification: Promotion ROI / lift analysis is a named loyalty reporting process: did the double-points weekend actually lift RevPAR? Linking performance_actuals to the active promotion enables incremental revenue
Reference to the property for which this daily revenue performance record is captured. Links to the property master entity in the Property domain.

Foreign key linking to fnb.revenue_center. Business justification: USALI mandates F&B performance reporting by revenue center (restaurant, bar, banquet). performance_actuals tracks fb_revenue but has no revenue_center FK. This link enables department-level P&L report

Foreign key linking to property.seasonal_calendar. Business justification: Performance actuals must be contextualized by season for meaningful YoY variance analysis (prior_year_revpar exists as a plain attr). Linking to seasonal_calendar enables season-adjusted STR benchmark

Average Daily Rate in the property's operating currency, calculated as total room revenue divided by rooms sold (excluding complimentary). Core KPI for STR STAR Report benchmarking, RGI, ARI, and MPI calculations.
Total ancillary revenue on the performance date from upsells, packages, resort fees, and add-on services not captured in departmental revenue lines. Supports total revenue and TRevPAR reporting.

Average Rate Index representing the property's ADR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. ARI > 1.0 indicates the property is achieving a rate premium over its comp set.
Number of rooms sold through direct channels (brand website, call center, walk-in) on the performance date. Supports direct vs. indirect channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.

business_glossary_term: Online Travel Agency (OTA) Channel Rooms Sold
description: Number of rooms sold through Online Travel Agency (OTA) channels (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com) on the performance date. Supports channel distribution mix analysis and OTA commission cost management.
Cost Per Occupied Room on the performance date, calculated as total rooms department operating expenses divided by rooms sold. Measures operational efficiency of the rooms department per USALI.
Timestamp when this performance actual record was first created in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.

business_glossary_term: Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) Contribution
description: Property-level EBITDA contribution on the performance date, representing operating earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. Used for ownership reporting, asset valuation, and management contract performance measurement.

Total Food and Beverage revenue generated on the performance date across all F&B outlets (restaurants, bars, banquets, in-room dining). Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA. Included in TRevPAR calculation per USALI.

Gross Operating Profit on the performance date, calculated as total operated department revenue minus total operated department expenses per USALI. Key profitability metric for ownership reporting and management contracts.

business_glossary_term: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR)
description: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as GOP divided by rooms available. Measures overall property profitability efficiency. Core KPI for ownership reporting and management fee calculations.

Indicates whether this daily performance actual record has been reconciled between Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA General Ledger. True = reconciled and approved for reporting; False = pending reconciliation. Supports financial close and SOX controls.
Total miscellaneous and rental income on the performance date including rentals, commissions, and other non-departmental income per USALI non-operating income classification.

Market Penetration Index representing the property's occupancy performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. MPI > 1.0 indicates the property is capturing more than its fair share of market occupancy.

Daily occupancy rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8250 = 82.50%), calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available. Core KPI for the STR STAR Report, DOR, and ownership reporting. Stored as a raw ratio; display layer applies percentage formatting.
Total parking revenue generated on the performance date from valet, self-park, and garage operations. Classified under other operated departments per USALI and included in TRevPAR.

The calendar date (business date) for which this revenue performance actual record applies. Represents the stay date or operating day used in the Daily Operations Report (DOR) and all KPI calculations.

business_glossary_term: Prior Year Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR)
description: RevPAR for the same calendar date in the prior year. Used for year-over-year (YOY) variance analysis in the DOR, STR STAR Report benchmarking, and ownership reporting.

Lifecycle status of this performance actual record indicating whether figures are preliminary (intra-day), final (end-of-day close), adjusted (post-close correction), or closed (period-locked). Supports audit and reconciliation workflows.

Revenue Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as ADR multiplied by occupancy rate (or total room revenue divided by rooms available). Primary rooms revenue KPI for ownership reporting, STR STAR Report, and RGI benchmarking.

Revenue Generation Index representing the property's RevPAR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. RGI > 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its comp set. Core competitive benchmarking KPI.

Total number of rooms available for sale on the performance date after deducting out-of-order (OOO) and out-of-service (OOS) rooms. Denominator for Occupancy Rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations per USALI and STR STAR Report standards.

Number of rooms occupied on a complimentary (no-charge) basis on the performance date. Tracked separately per USALI for accurate ADR and revenue reporting, as complimentary rooms are excluded from ADR calculation.
Number of rooms designated as out-of-order (OOO) on the performance date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Excluded from rooms available per USALI inventory standards.
Total number of rooms occupied (sold) on the performance date, including complimentary rooms. Used to calculate Occupancy Rate (OCC) and Average Daily Rate (ADR) per USALI standards.
Number of rooms sold under negotiated contract rates (airline crew, long-stay corporate contracts) on the performance date. Supports contract segment revenue analysis and channel mix reporting.
Number of rooms sold to group accounts (MICE, corporate groups, tour groups) on the performance date. Supports group vs. transient mix analysis and group revenue contribution reporting per USALI.
Number of rooms sold to transient (individual/FIT) guests on the performance date. Supports segment-level revenue analysis and channel mix reporting per USALI market segmentation standards.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this performance actual record was sourced or reconciled (e.g., Oracle OPERA PMS for rooms data, SAP S/4HANA for financial totals). Supports data lineage and reconciliation audits.
Total spa and wellness revenue generated on the performance date. Included in total property revenue and TRevPAR calculation per USALI other operated departments classification.

Total gross revenue across all operated departments and income streams on the performance date (rooms + F&B + spa + parking + other operated departments + miscellaneous + ancillary). Denominator basis for TRevPAR. Reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL per USALI.

Total gross room revenue generated on the performance date across all rate plans and channels, before deductions. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL. Numerator for ADR and RevPAR calculations per USALI.

Total Revenue Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as total property revenue divided by rooms available. Measures total revenue productivity of the property beyond rooms. Core KPI for ownership and management reporting.
Timestamp when this performance actual record was last modified, including post-close adjustments or reconciliation corrections. Supports audit trail and change tracking.
Unique surrogate identifier for a Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) record in the revenue management system. Primary key for this entity.

Identifier of the demand forecast record from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS that this forecast-based Dynamic Rate Rule references for its trigger evaluation.

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific channels differently (e.g., OTA rates adjust based on commission costs, direct channel gets best rate). RMS systems apply channel-specific rate optimization strat

Identifier of the local demand event (e.g., MICE event, conference, festival, sporting event) that triggers this event-based Dynamic Rate Rule. Links to the event master record in Delphi by Amadeus or the events domain.

Foreign key linking to guest.segment. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific guest segments for personalized rate adjustments. Revenue management systems apply segment-specific pricing logic (e.g., loyalty tier discounts, corporate negotia

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules adjust rates based on member tier for personalized revenue optimization. Business process: applying tier-specific rate adjustments in revenue management systems to reward high-va

Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.
Identifier of the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all eligible rate plans for the property.
Identifier of the specific room type (e.g., standard king, deluxe suite) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all room types at the property.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Dynamic rules can target specific high-value members for personalized pricing strategies. Business process: VIP member-specific rate rules for retention and revenue optimization, common in luxury hosp

Indicates whether the Dynamic Rate Rule increases or decreases the rate when triggered. Provides explicit directional intent separate from the sign of adjustment_value to prevent ambiguity in RMS configuration.

Method by which the Dynamic Rate Rule adjusts the published rate when triggered. Percentage applies a relative increase or decrease to the current rate; absolute_amount adds or subtracts a fixed currency value; bar_offset moves the rate a fixed amount above or below the Best Available Rate (BAR); bar_multiplier applies a multiplier to the BAR.

The magnitude of the rate adjustment applied when the Dynamic Rate Rule is triggered. Interpreted according to adjustment_type: a value of 0.10 with type 'percentage' means a 10% rate increase; a value of 25.00 with type 'absolute_amount' means a $25 rate increase; a value of -15.00 with type 'bar_offset' means $15 below BAR.
Maximum number of days in advance of the arrival date within which a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Null indicates no upper advance booking restriction.
Minimum number of days in advance of the arrival date that a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Supports advance purchase pricing strategies.
Workflow approval state of the Dynamic Rate Rule indicating whether it has been reviewed and authorized by a revenue manager before activation. Supports SOX-compliant pricing governance controls.
Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved this Dynamic Rate Rule for activation. Required for SOX financial controls audit trail on pricing decisions.
Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was approved for activation by the authorized revenue manager. Part of the pricing governance audit trail required under SOX financial controls.
Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail for pricing configuration governance.

Seven-character binary string (positions 1–7 = Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this Dynamic Rate Rule is eligible to fire. A value of '1111100' means Monday through Friday only. Enables day-of-week yield optimization strategies.
The calendar date from which this Dynamic Rate Rule becomes active and eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments in the RMS. Aligns with the stay date range for yield optimization.
The calendar date on which this Dynamic Rate Rule expires and ceases to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no defined expiry.

Indicates whether this Dynamic Rate Rule can be combined (stacked) with other active rules for the same stay date, room type, and rate plan. When False, this rule takes exclusive precedence based on rule_priority and no other rules are applied concurrently.

Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was most recently evaluated as true and triggered an automated pricing adjustment in the RMS. Used by revenue managers to monitor rule activity and effectiveness.
Maximum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights beyond which this Dynamic Rate Rule does not apply. Null indicates no upper LOS restriction.
Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights that must be satisfied for this Dynamic Rate Rule to apply. Used in LOS-based yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods.

The highest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from exceeding the property's maximum acceptable rate cap. Sensitive pricing strategy data.

The lowest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from falling below the property's minimum acceptable ADR threshold. Sensitive pricing strategy data.
Number of days in the booking pickup window used to evaluate pickup velocity for pickup-based Dynamic Rate Rules. For example, a value of 7 means the rule evaluates rooms booked in the last 7 days.

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Rule Reference ID
description: Native rule identifier assigned by the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) for this Dynamic Rate Rule. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational RMS.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the Dynamic Rate Rule within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference between IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, and Sabre SynXis CRS.
Detailed narrative description of the Dynamic Rate Rule's business purpose, pricing logic, and intended yield optimization outcome.
Human-readable name describing the Dynamic Rate Rule, used by revenue managers to identify and manage pricing strategies within the RMS dashboard.

Numeric priority ranking that determines the order of evaluation when multiple Dynamic Rate Rules are eligible to fire simultaneously for the same property, room type, and rate plan. Lower numeric values indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority).

Current lifecycle state of the Dynamic Rate Rule governing whether it is eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Active rules are evaluated by the RMS engine; draft rules are under configuration; suspended rules are temporarily disabled; archived rules are retained for historical audit.

Classification of the Dynamic Rate Rule by the trigger mechanism driving automated pricing adjustments. Occupancy-based rules fire on OCC thresholds; pickup-based on booking velocity; competitive-based on RGI/ARI/MPI benchmarks from STR STAR Report; event-based on MICE or local demand events; LOS-based on Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions; forecast-based on RMS demand forecasts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: occupancy_based|pickup_based|competitive_based|event_based|los_based|forecast_based — promote to reference product]
Cumulative number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule has been triggered and applied a pricing adjustment since its activation date. Used to assess rule utilization and yield optimization effectiveness.

Comparison operator applied to the trigger threshold value to determine whether the rule condition is met. For example, 'greater_than' with a threshold of 0.80 means the rule fires when OCC exceeds 80%.

Unit of measure for the trigger threshold value. Percentage applies to OCC-based rules; room_count applies to pickup-based rules; index_value applies to competitive benchmarking rules (RGI, MPI, ARI); currency_amount applies to ADR-based triggers; nights applies to LOS-based rules.

The numeric threshold at which this Dynamic Rate Rule fires. For occupancy-based rules, this is the OCC percentage (e.g., 0.80 = 80%). For pickup-based rules, this is the number of rooms picked up within the pickup window. For competitive-based rules, this is the RGI or ARI index value.
Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer ETL pipeline.
Incremental version counter tracking the number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule configuration has been modified. Supports audit trail and change management for pricing strategy governance.
